hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a purpose-built two-storey Commercial building that reflects vernacular interpretations of early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s pattern of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. The property has retained its original form and many building features including rug-brick cladding and second storey window openings with brick voussoirs and pediment. Anchoring the northeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Baldwin Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Baldwin Street. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 23. 235 Augusta Avenue 200 Baldwin Street 202 Baldwin Street 204 Baldwin Street 206 Baldwin Street 29 DCH | FO :D FO YTIC 5202 243 & 245 Augusta Avenue (R-L) 243 & 245 AUGUSTA AVENUE

photo_observations.cornice_details: Plain metal coping and shallow roof edge at the top of the brick facade.

facade_description: Two-storey warm red brick facade with a cross-gable roof and parapet, approximately 5.2 m wide, built flush to the sidewalk. Photo observation shows three narrow white-trimmed second-floor windows above a wide glazed storefront (Nomnom Crunch) with a bright red sign band and black framing. The upper facade is intact but tagged in places. A skylight/dome is visible behind the facade line at the roofline. A purpose-built 1914-1930 commercial building retaining its original form, rug-brick cladding, and second-storey window openings with brick voussoirs. The original shopfront has been replaced with contemporary glazing and branded signage.
heritage_summary: The property has value as a purpose-built two-storey Commercial building that reflects vernacular interpretations of early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Summary: Reference Primary Address Entrance Address(es) Number 23.
